codeReview.summary: The shared hotDataRevision invalidation closes the main cache-coherence gap between `/simple/price` and `/coins/markets`, and the added tests cover refresh-driven invalidation plus stale-policy flips. However, the reviewed commit leaves the no-runtime bootstrap path in `src/app.ts` without a revision bump when initial sync first makes hot data visible, so one endpoint can still serve a stale cached generation across that startup transition while the other recomputes.

issues.description: In the `startBackgroundJobs: false` bootstrap path, `initialSyncCompleted` is set directly after `runInitialMarketSync()` without advancing `marketDataRuntimeState.hotDataRevision`. Any `/simple/price` or `/coins/markets` response cached before readiness can therefore survive the initial-sync-complete transition until TTL expiry, recreating the cross-endpoint contradiction this feature is meant to prevent. The later fix in commit `d1e3e8a` confirms this hole was real.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Hot-endpoint cache coherence depends on every visibility/freshness policy transition advancing `hotDataRevision`, including the no-runtime `onReady` bootstrap path in `src/app.ts`, not just background runtime refresh/failure transitions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/services/market-runtime.ts` bumps the revision for runtime-driven success/failure transitions, while this reviewed commit did not yet bump it in `src/app.ts` after `runInitialMarketSync()` when `startBackgroundJobs` is false. A follow-up feature `hot-data-revision-startup-transition-fix` was later added to address exactly this gap.

summary: Fail: the feature substantially improves cache-transition coherence, but it misses the no-runtime startup transition in `src/app.ts`, leaving a blocking stale-cache path for the same shared hot endpoints until the later startup-transition fix.
